title: Treehouse Of Horror Xviii

jokes.text: Hello. Remember Halloween? It was last week. But here at the Simpson house, we're still celebrating.
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging the show is airing late but they're still doing Halloween anyway
jokes.explanation: Self-aware meta humor about TV scheduling, economical and functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: Excuse me. Now, our first gory story is... Oh, for crying out loud!
jokes.setup: Trying to introduce the first story
jokes.punchline: Getting interrupted by visual interference
jokes.explanation: Standard TV interference gag, functional but predictable

jokes.text: No fair. Can't anyone just watch the show they're watching?
jokes.punchline: Breaking fourth wall to complain about viewers channel surfing
jokes.explanation: Clever meta commentary on modern viewing habits, good economy

jokes.text: Mmm. Developed by.
jokes.punchline: Homer appreciating the taste of credits text that appears on screen
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character comedy - he'd taste anything. Brilliant visual/dialogue integration with minimal words

jokes.text: Crème brulée! Or, in English: Burnt cream!
jokes.punchline: Translating the fancy French name to mundane English description
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about pretentious food names, fits Marge's practical nature

jokes.text: Bart, go out to the shed and get some more butane! ...butane this.
jokes.setup: Marge asking Bart to get butane
jokes.punchline: Homer making terrible pun on 'but then'
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer dad joke - terrible pun delivered with confidence, fits character perfectly

jokes.text: What's that unearthly glow? The only sensible thing to do is explore it on my own.
jokes.punchline: Deciding to investigate alone, the classic horror movie mistake
jokes.explanation: Good horror trope subversion - Bart acknowledges he's making the obvious bad choice

jokes.text: Is that your space name? No, you hit me in my 700 testicles.
jokes.punchline: Alien has an absurd number of testicles that got hit
jokes.explanation: Absurd alien anatomy joke with shock value, memorable and quotable

jokes.text: My friends and I came in peace to find your vulnerabilities and, um, shore them up with more peace. Yes.
jokes.punchline: Obviously lying about peaceful intentions with terrible cover story
jokes.explanation: Classic alien deception trope with poor execution making it funny

jokes.text: Is that a ray gun? No, it's a... deodorant applicator.
jokes.punchline: Alien's ridiculous excuse for ray gun
jokes.explanation: Standard alien disguise gag, functional but predictable

jokes.text: Smells like a summer breeze.
jokes.punchline: It actually works as deodorant
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the deodorant lie - turns out it actually is deodorant

jokes.text: Jim Halterman bobblehead doll. He's a local car dealer.
jokes.punchline: Having a bobblehead of random local car dealer
jokes.explanation: Specific, weird detail that feels authentically Bart - collecting random local memorabilia

jokes.text: do all humans have such weak necks or just the one you call 'Jim Halterman'?
jokes.punchline: Assuming all humans have bobblehead physics
jokes.explanation: Clever alien perspective joke - misunderstanding human anatomy based on novelty item

jokes.text: I only hope those scientists leave me my mouth, so I may spread my message of peace through song. Peace... Peace, peace, peace From space.
jokes.punchline: Terrible improvised peace song
jokes.explanation: Alien's awkward attempt at seeming peaceful through bad music

jokes.text: seven billion body bags... Can I hammer that? No. Can I weld that? No! Can I tighten the... I said no!
jokes.setup: Alien listing suspicious items including body bags
jokes.punchline: Homer obsessed with using his tools despite context
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - completely missing obvious red flags, focused only on tools

jokes.text: I'm helping! I'm helping!
jokes.punchline: Homer's childlike enthusiasm for meaningless task
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer naivety - genuine excitement about being included

jokes.text: They were in yesterday's New York Times.
jokes.setup: Alien asking for secret missile locations
jokes.punchline: Military secrets are published in newspaper
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire about government transparency failures

jokes.text: We will cook them, we will eat them 'Cause that's the way to treat them...
jokes.punchline: Singing about it in upbeat musical style
jokes.explanation: Dark content made humorous through cheerful musical delivery

jokes.text: Now, how about the neck of my butt?
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical anatomy reference
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer crude humor with made-up body part

jokes.text: Someone's taking the highway to the danger zone.
jokes.punchline: Top Gun reference in sexual context
jokes.explanation: Standard pop culture reference without much twist

jokes.text: Why am I getting words in edgewise?
jokes.setup: Homer realizing something is wrong with Marge
jokes.punchline: Noticing he can actually speak during intimacy
jokes.explanation: Clever way to show Homer realizes something's wrong - he's getting to talk

jokes.text: It's because I'm Jewish.
jokes.punchline: Alien assumes it's antisemitism
jokes.explanation: Unexpected angle on alien discrimination - projecting human prejudice

jokes.text: I got a plan to save your space doggy.
jokes.punchline: Calling the alien 'space doggy'
jokes.explanation: Homer's childlike way of addressing the alien threat

jokes.text: Oh, I'm not decent. Let me throw something on.
jokes.punchline: Homer in drag answering door
jokes.explanation: Standard cross-dressing disguise gag, predictable but functional

jokes.text: Can I help you, gentlemen? Uh, we know it's you, Mr. Simpson.
jokes.setup: Homer in obvious disguise answering door
jokes.punchline: Agents immediately see through terrible disguise
jokes.explanation: Classic visual comedy setup - Homer's incompetence at deception

jokes.text: Ha! You missed 'em. Bart and our alien friend are hundreds of miles away by now.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming to have fooled agents
jokes.punchline: Immediately revealing exactly where they are
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer stupidity - thinks he's being clever while giving everything away

jokes.text: More of me to love.
jokes.punchline: Alien's positive spin on being heavy
jokes.explanation: Generic fat joke response, nothing special

jokes.text: The South shall rise again.
jokes.punchline: Random Confederate battle cry
jokes.explanation: Random historical reference without clear connection to context

jokes.text: Well done, Columbo. That's right. We watch Columbo. It's on during rain-outs of gleep-glop games.
jokes.setup: Bart stating obvious about killing agents
jokes.punchline: Alien referencing detective show and explaining alien sports
jokes.explanation: Good alien cultural detail with Earth TV reference

jokes.text: Foolish biped. It's not a phone. It's a space portal. Our shock troops will teleport in and eat everyone's heads
jokes.setup: Kodos seemingly calling home for help
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's actually an invasion portal
jokes.explanation: Good reveal of alien's true plan, effective misdirection payoff

jokes.text: They're gonna let us kill one!
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic about participating in murder
jokes.explanation: Dark humor from Bart's amoral excitement about violence

jokes.text: I guess you should judge a book by its cover. Definitely, especially if you count the inside flap as part of the cover. It usually gives you a great idea of what the book's about.
jokes.setup: Homer saying you should judge books by covers
jokes.punchline: Lisa's overly literal interpretation about book jacket flaps
jokes.explanation: Lisa's pedantic response to Homer's lesson is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Actually, I'm still alive, so technically, it's vivisection. No one likes a know-it-all.
jokes.punchline: Alien correcting terminology then getting shut down
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about pedantic corrections, with Homer delivering perfect comeback

jokes.text: The problem is you don't share my interest in not being with you.
jokes.punchline: Marge's blunt statement about not wanting to be together
jokes.explanation: Perfectly structured relationship joke with economic phrasing

jokes.text: Why does he always bring up my weight?
jokes.punchline: Marge interpreting number as weight reference
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's insecurity

jokes.text: I'm going to, uh... Midnight Monkey Madness at the zoo. Well, I won't be home, either. I'm, um... flipping over wheelbarrows in case it rains.
jokes.setup: Couple making excuses to avoid each other
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous fake activities
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of absurd excuses, both characters trying to out-weird each other

jokes.text: Have fun at your crazy-sounding thing. And you have fun at your preposterous event.
jokes.setup: Couple acknowledging each other's fake plans
jokes.punchline: Both calling out the absurdity while maintaining the lie
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff showing both know the other is lying but maintain facade

jokes.text: Now all I have to do is think of a clever line before I pull the trigger. Oh, that's a perfect one. But I don't need to say it out loud 'cause I'm by myself.
jokes.setup: Marge as assassin preparing to shoot
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging action movie tropes but not following through
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on action movie clichés with perfect Marge practicality

jokes.text: Out of my way, mystery skank!
jokes.punchline: Marge using harsh language uncharacteristically
jokes.explanation: Shocking to hear Marge use such language, perfect character subversion

jokes.text: Marge is a professional assassin!
jokes.punchline: Homer's shocked realization about his wife
jokes.explanation: Plot revelation moment. Functions more as story beat than standalone comedy.

jokes.text: You just can't get Russian gangster blood out. Must be something they eat.
jokes.punchline: Practical observation about ethnic blood differences
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with Marge's practical homemaker perspective

jokes.text: A blender cozy. How was your Midnight Monkey Madness?
jokes.punchline: Marge's domestic cover story and callback to ridiculous excuse
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier cover stories. 'Blender cozy' continues domestic assassin theme.

jokes.text: I turned over so many wheelbarrows... Wait. That was your thing.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to maintain lie about his evening
jokes.punchline: Mixing up their fake stories
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Homer's confusion about their lies

jokes.text: It's poison! Whatever you do, don't eat it. Okay, you're already eating it, but don't finish it. Okay, you finished it, but don't ask for... Seconds, please.
jokes.setup: Marge realizing she served Homer poisoned food
jokes.punchline: Homer eating despite warnings and asking for more
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalating structure with Homer's unstoppable eating habits overriding poison concern

jokes.text: Okay, you're already eating it, but don't finish it. Okay, you finished it, but don't ask for... Seconds, please.
jokes.setup: Marge trying to stop Homer from eating poison
jokes.punchline: Homer ignoring warnings and asking for more
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalating structure showing Homer's food obsession overriding self-preservation

jokes.text: You moron! Just kill her! I'll kill her after dessert!
jokes.setup: Boss ordering Homer to kill Marge
jokes.punchline: Homer prioritizing dessert over assassination
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer priorities - food comes before murder, even of his wife

jokes.text: How could you not tell me you were an assassin? How could you not tell me? I told you 20 times! You never listen!
jokes.punchline: Marge claiming she told Homer repeatedly about being a killer
jokes.explanation: Perfect marriage humor. Homer's inattentiveness applies even to major secrets like murder.

jokes.text: Well, they're not as pretty as you, Marge.
jokes.punchline: Homer's sweet but inappropriate compliment
jokes.explanation: Homer's sweet nature coming through even in murder context

jokes.text: I just get to keep whatever's in the guy's wallet.
jokes.punchline: Homer's much smaller compensation
jokes.explanation: Good economic joke about Homer getting the worse deal

jokes.text: I was out getting drunk, then killing people!
jokes.setup: Marge accusing Homer of drinking instead of killing
jokes.punchline: Homer clarifying he does both
jokes.explanation: Homer's matter-of-fact correction about his murder routine

jokes.text: That's my favorite couch! Why do you think I'm kicking it?
jokes.setup: Marge complaining about Homer kicking couch
jokes.punchline: Homer's perfect logic for targeting her favorite furniture
jokes.explanation: Excellent domestic dispute logic - Homer knows exactly how to hurt her

jokes.text: Stop it! That's my favorite couch! Why do you think I'm kicking it?
jokes.punchline: Homer's targeted destruction of Marge's favorite things
jokes.explanation: Homer's petty revenge tactics. Strategic property damage during domestic dispute.

jokes.text: Are you guys fighting over us? Whatever we did, we're sorry.
jokes.punchline: Kids assuming they caused domestic violence
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about kids' perspective on parental conflict

jokes.text: Great! Now they're going to have tummy aches tomorrow. And where will 'Super Dad' be then? At your funeral.
jokes.setup: Marge complaining about Homer giving kids ice cream
jokes.punchline: Escalating to murder threat over parenting dispute
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from domestic complaint to murder threat

jokes.text: Oh, that was so hot! I love watching you do other guys! Mmm, I'd like to see you do a guy sometime.
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo about murder
jokes.explanation: Standard double entendre mixing violence and sex

jokes.text: I would have taken a bribe. Oh, that was so hot! I love watching you do other guys!
jokes.setup: Police corruption and Homer watching Marge fight
jokes.punchline: Homer's sexual excitement from watching wife's violence
jokes.explanation: Homer's inappropriate arousal from violence. Dark humor through sexual excitement at wife's fighting.

jokes.text: killing people together has really spiced things up in the bedroom.
jokes.punchline: Murder improving their sex life
jokes.explanation: Dark relationship humor about shared hobbies

jokes.text: Think of me on Pizza Fridays.
jokes.setup: Skinner being killed by assassin parents
jokes.punchline: Mundane last words about school lunch
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific last words - perfectly banal for Skinner

jokes.text: I got your candy right here.
jokes.punchline: Agnes implying violence instead of treats
jokes.explanation: Standard cranky old lady threat, fits Agnes perfectly

jokes.text: 'Trick or Treat' isn't just some phrase you chant mindlessly like The Lord's Prayer. It's an oral contract!
jokes.setup: Kids deciding how to respond to no candy
jokes.punchline: Bart treating Halloween as legal contract
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Bart logic combining legal concepts with Halloween traditions, plus religious dig

jokes.text: Can't talk. Lighting poo.
jokes.punchline: Nelson busy with classic prank
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson moment - economical and perfectly in character

jokes.text: ! taem ttub-gip ekilsllems daeh ruoY
jokes.punchline: Bart speaking backwards (Your head smells like pig-butt meat!)
jokes.explanation: Backwards speech gag - novelty value but requires work to decode

jokes.text: I've got something that will scare the H-E-double sippy straws out of them.
jokes.setup: Ned offering to help with Halloween kids
jokes.punchline: Ned's overcautious curse word euphemism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned Flanders overcensoring - 'sippy straws' for 'hockey sticks'

jokes.text: I fed your fish. You overfed them! You're the worst one of all!
jokes.punchline: Homer's completely disproportionate fish-feeding crime
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke - Homer somehow makes fish care the worst offense

jokes.text: Sin gets us something? Awesome! I was just in it for the sin.
jokes.punchline: Kids excited about getting rewards for sinning
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of religious moral lesson - kids miss the point entirely

jokes.text: Welcome to Heck House! You're about to discover what a life of sin will get you! Sin gets us something? Awesome! I was just in it for the sin.
jokes.punchline: Bart excited about potential sin rewards
jokes.explanation: Bart's complete misunderstanding of moral lesson. Perfect character response to religious warning.

jokes.text: He died as he lived--like a dork.
jokes.punchline: Nelson's callous assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson cruelty - dismissive of death and life equally

jokes.text: May I have a clean American newspaper, please, no 'Doonesbury'? Wouldn't you rather have a bikini magazine? I guess a little peek won't hurt me. No! He died as he lived--like a dork.
jokes.punchline: Milhouse dies for looking at magazine, epitaph insults him
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific death for Milhouse with appropriate insult

jokes.text: Please, Lord, grant me the power to psychologically torture them into loving you.
jokes.setup: Ned praying for help with kids
jokes.punchline: Asking God for torture powers for conversion
jokes.explanation: Sharp religious satire - Ned's sincere prayer for psychological torture capabilities

jokes.text: Oh, why did I take pride in my child's accomplishments?
jokes.setup: Hell house showing sin of pride
jokes.punchline: Homer regretting normal parental feeling
jokes.explanation: Good parenting humor - Homer feeling guilty about natural parental pride

jokes.text: I thought I was killed by that magic spaghetti!
jokes.setup: Homer in sloth section of hell house
jokes.punchline: Homer's bizarre interpretation of his laziness death
jokes.explanation: Absurd Homer logic about his demise - 'magic spaghetti' is wonderfully nonsensical

jokes.text: Oh, how I envy the crotchless.
jokes.punchline: Bizarre specific envy of anatomical condition
jokes.explanation: Absurdist envy example. The specificity of 'crotchless' makes it memorably bizarre.

jokes.text: Oh, how I envy the crotchless.
jokes.punchline: Homer's inappropriate envy statement
jokes.explanation: Sexual innuendo masquerading as religious commentary, economical but cheap

jokes.text: I keep telling you: I'm Hindu! I regret... nothing.
jokes.punchline: Apu pointing out wrong religion, Comic Book Guy's defiance
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moments - Apu's logical objection, Comic Book Guy's stubborn defiance

jokes.text: I just want to say that for watching this network, you're all going to hell! And that includes FX, Fox Sports, and our newest Devil's portal, The Wall Street Journal.
jokes.punchline: Show attacking its own network and corporate siblings
jokes.explanation: Bold corporate satire - The Simpsons attacking Fox and News Corp properties

tmdb_overview: The series' 18th Halloween show features three vignettes that parody movies: one that spoofs "Mr. & Mrs. Smith"; one that sends up "E.T."; and one that parodies "Se7en."
